title: draconia campestris subsp. glutinosa
intro: Draconia campestris subsp. glutinosa, also known as the wild gladiolus or desert cannas, is a perennial plant species in the Iridaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Draconia campestris subsp. glutinosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is typically harvested from the wild or cultivated in controlled environments.
